title: Git Tutorial
content:
Git#Version control basics and Git setup guide.
Git Installation on Windows#This guide walks you through installing Git on a Windows machine.
Download Git#Visit the official Git website: https://git-scm.com Click the Download for Windows button The installer will automatically detect your system (32-bit or 64-bit) and download the appropriate version Run the Installer#Open the downloaded .exe file Click Next to proceed through the installation wizard Installation Options#Step 1: Select Components#Keep the default selections checked: ✓ Git Bash Here ✓ Git GUI Here ✓ Git LFS (Large File Support) ✓ Associate .git* configuration files with the default text editor Step 2: Choose Default Editor#Select your preferred text editor (Notepad++, VS Code, or Vim) If unsure, choose Nano as it’s beginner-friendly Step 3: Configure PATH Environment#Select Git from the command line and also from 3rd-party software This allows you to use Git in any terminal application Step 4: HTTPS Transport Backend#Select Use the native Windows Secure Channel library This uses Windows’ built-in SSL/TLS for secure connections Step 5: Line Ending Conversions#Select Checkout as-is, commit as-is (recommended for cross-platform projects) Or select Checkout Windows-style, commit Unix-style if you’re working on Windows only Step 6: Terminal Emulator#Select Use Windows’ default console window MinTTY is also a good option if you prefer a Unix-like terminal Step 7: Extra Options#Check Enable file system caching for better performance Check Enable Git Credential Manager for easier authentication Complete Installation#Click Install to start the installation Wait for the process to complete (usually takes 1-2 minutes) Click Finish Verify Installation#Open Command Prompt or PowerShell and run:
git --versionYou should see output like: git version 2.43.0.windows.1
Initial Configuration#Configure your Git identity for commits:
git config --global user.name "Your Name" git config --global user.email "your.email@example.com"Verify the configuration:
git config --global --listNext Steps#Learn the basic Git commands Set up a GitHub or GitLab account if you haven’t already Clone your first repository and start version control!

title: Basics of HTML and CSS
content:
Basics of HTML and CSS#Disclaimer#All the steps and explanations in this documentation are written specifically for users on the Windows operating system.
While Git itself is cross-platform, certain installation steps, paths, and UI elements may differ on Linux or macOS.
Choosing a Text Editor#Before working with Git or documentation files, a text editor is required. A text editor is essential for:
Writing documentation (Markdown / HTML) Editing configuration files Managing project files efficiently Some commonly used text editors on Windows include:
Visual Studio Code Sublime Text Notepad++ Atom Selecting a Text Editor#For my workflow, I decided to use Visual Studio Code (VS Code) because:
I am already familiar with it It has a clean and intuitive interface It offers excellent Git integration It supports extensions for almost every language However, you are free to choose any text editor you are comfortable with.
Downloading Visual Studio Code#To download Visual Studio Code:
Open Google Chrome Go to the official Visual Studio Code website Download the latest Windows .exe installer Always make sure to download the installer from the official website.
Installing Visual Studio Code#During installation, most default options can be kept unchanged.
However, ensure that the following option is selected:
✅ Add “Open with Code” action to Windows Explorer This allows Visual Studio Code to be opened directly from the File Explorer using the right-click menu.
After installation, Visual Studio Code is ready for use.
Installing Useful VS Code Extensions#After installing Visual Studio Code, the next step is to install a few helpful extensions that make web development and documentation easier.
Live Server Extension#The Live Server extension allows you to:
Launch a local development server Automatically refresh the browser when files are saved Steps to install:
Open Visual Studio Code Go to the Extensions tab (Ctrl + Shift + X) Search for Live Server Click Install Save Typing / Auto Save#To avoid losing changes and to improve workflow, Auto Save can be enabled:
Click on File Select Auto Save This ensures files are saved automatically while typing.
Getting Started with HTML#With the editor and extensions ready, the next step is to try the basics of HTML (HyperText Markup Language).
Creating a New Project in VS Code#Open Visual Studio Code Click on File → Open Folder Create a new folder and give it a name (for example: html-basics) Select the folder and click Open This folder will act as your project workspace.
Creating a New HTML File#Inside the opened folder, create a new file Name the file index.html Basic HTML Structure#Below is a basic HTML template:
<!DOCTYPE html> <html> <head> <title>Page Title</title> </head> <body> <h1>This is a Heading</h1> <p>This is a paragraph.</p> </body> </html>

title: About Me
content:
About Me#Hello! I’m Nadec Biju, a Mechanical Engineering student at CUSAT (2022-2026) passionate about digital fabrication, robotics, and innovative product development.
I’ve always been drawn to the moment when an idea stops being just a thought and starts becoming something real.
For as long as I can remember, I’ve loved taking things apart, understanding how they work, and building them back better. Making, breaking, fixing, and redesigning is where I feel most comfortable. Over the years, this interest has grown into building real projects like off-road machines, hydrogen buggies, mechanical systems, and many CAD models that started as rough sketches and became working parts.
CAD is where my imagination gets shape. It is where messy ideas turn into clean designs, proper measurements, and mechanisms that actually work in the real world.
I enjoy solving problems that do not come with instructions, designing things that do not exist yet, and pushing projects from “what if” to “it works.” For me, engineering is not just about formulas or software. It is about creating things that move, improve, fail, and finally do something meaningful.
Skills#CAD & Design: Solidworks, Fusion 360 | Fabrication: 3D Printing, Laser Cutting, CNC | Programming: Python, ESP32, Arduino
Certifications#🏆 SOLIDWORKS Additive Manufacturing Associate – Dassault Systèmes 🏆 SOLIDWORKS CAD Design Associate – Dassault Systèmes 🏆 SOLIDWORKS Electrical Design Associate – Dassault Systèmes 🏆 SOLIDWORKS Sustainability Associate – Dassault Systèmes Gallery#❮❯Contact#📞 +91 7558804529 | 📍 Kochi, Kerala | 📧 nadecbiju6@gmail.com | 💼 LinkedIn

title: Agreement
content:
Fab Academy Student Agreement#The Fab Academy is responsible for:#Teaching principles and practices of digital fabrication Arranging lectures, recitations, meetings, and events for the class Evaluating and providing feedback on student work Offering clear standards for completing assignments Certifying and archiving student progress Supervising class preparation Reviewing prospective students, instructors, and labs Providing central staff and infrastructure for students, instructors, and labs Fund-raising for costs not covered by student tuition Managing and reporting on the program’s finances, results, and impacts Publicizing the program Promoting a respectful environment free of harassment and discrimination Encouraging a diverse, accessible, and equitable community I am a Fab Academy student, responsible for:#Attending class lectures and participating in reviews Developing and documenting projects assigned to introduce and demonstrate skills, in enough detail to be able to reproduce them Honestly reporting on my work, and appropriately attributing the work of others (both human and machine, including AI prompts) Being prepared to answer questions about my work Allowing the Fab Academy to share my work (with attribution) in the class for purposes compatible with its mission Working safely Leaving workspaces in the same (or better) condition than I found them Participating in the upkeep of my lab Ensuring that my tuition for local and central class costs is covered Following locally applicable health and safety guidance Recognizing limits on lab and staff time Promoting a respectful environment free of harassment and discrimination Signed by committing this file in my repository:
Nadec Biju
View Student Agreement Document

title: Week 01 - Project management
content:
Week 01 - Principles and Practices , Project management#Week 01 marks the beginning of Fab Academy 2026. This week focuses on understanding the structure of the course and learning the fundamentals of project management, which involves planning, organizing, and tracking work effectively over time.
Rather than making physical artifacts, this week is about setting up a workflow that will be used consistently throughout the program.
What I Learned This Week#During this week, I explored the following topics:
Version control systems (Git and GitHub) Learning how to track changes, manage versions, and maintain a clean repository for documentation and files.
Basics of HTML and CSS Understanding the fundamentals required to build and customize a personal documentation website.
Project management tools and techniques Learning how to plan work, break down tasks, and manage time efficiently across weekly assignments.
Getting Started With Documentation Understanding Fab Academy documentation standards and how to clearly communicate process, progress, and learning.
Assignments#The assignments for this week included:
• Download, read, and sign the student agreement, and commit the file to my repository
• Completing a Git tutorial to understand version control basics
• Creating a personal website in the Fab Academy class archive
• Planning and sketching ideas for a potential final project
The planning of the week#Day Activity WED Review of the class with Neil THU Git + Website FRI About me SAT Sketch Final Project + Global open time SUN Documentation MON Recitation: version control TUE Regional Review This schedule helped balance learning new tools while keeping documentation up to date.
Tools Used#Software: Git GitHub VS Code HTML/CSS Reflection#This week helped me understand that Fab Academy is not just about completing assignments, but about building a disciplined workflow. Proper version control, structured documentation, and time management are essential skills that directly affect the quality of future work.
Establishing these habits early will make it easier to handle more complex fabrication, electronics, and programming tasks in the coming weeks.
